item.title: Nullish Checking in Javascript
item.description: Null checks are a common need in programs and it's clunky to do them in Javascript, unless you use the "valueOf" trick

item.title: Lists, Lists, Lists
item.description: Lists as data structures, presentational design and real life things.

item.title: Using the CSS Mix Blend Mode for dark/light theme
item.description: CSS offers a nifty mix blend mode feature which can handle giving text a contrasting color based on the background color.

item.title: React's useEffect as an "Event Listener"
item.description: Learning useEffect can be challenging to newcomers to React, but teaching it as an analog to addEventListener can make it easier

item.title: Variadic Functions in Javascript
item.description: An example of variadic functions in JS

item.title: Generating UUID's in the Browser
item.description: A clever way to generate random UUID's in modern Browser's

item.title: Composition over Inheritance and Revisiting the Null Object Pattern
item.description: Composition over Inheritance and Revisiting the Null Object Pattern

item.title: Javascript's Immutability Toolkit
item.description: A conversation about methods native to Javascript that conserve immutability of data.

item.title: Lookup-Table Syntax Options in Javascript
item.description: Let's see a few ways to create lookup-tables in Javascript

item.title: The Null Object Pattern in the Functional Style
item.description: Let's see how to create a Null Object pattern in Javascript

item.title: The Good and Bad Parts of JS (according to Kyle Simpson)
item.description: Notes of the good parts of Javascript and the parts to avoid according to Kyle Simpson's books and lectures.

item.description: Notes of the good parts of Javascript and the parts to avoid according to Douglas Crockford's books and lectures.

item.title: Chained CSS Classes - A Proposal
item.description: A thought experiment on what BEM CSS could look like as simply chained classes...

item.title: Simple State Reducer With Objects, Switch-Case & Generators
item.description: Let's build a super simple State Reducer in three different ways
